about_text:
DesktopCreator

  Programm developed
  using Python 3 + FreeSimpleGUI
  and compiled with Docker
  for backward compatibility.
  Author: Yves Le Chevalier
  For: My Human Kit
  {version}
help_text:
Program for creating or modifying a Linux launcher.

  The default location to create a launcher (so it appears in the Linux menu) is:
  ~/.local/share/applications

  You can choose another location (e.g. Desktop), but in that case the application will
  not appear in the menu.
  Use the 'Change Folder' button to select a different location.

  If the selected folder contains launchers with uppercase letters in their names,
  a non-blocking warning will be displayed.
  You can disable this warning using 'Don't show again'.
  To re-enable it, use 'Enable case-sensitivity errors' in the File menu.

  Enter the launcher name (without '.desktop'), then click OK.

  If a launcher with the same name already exists:
  - choose a different name, or
  - edit the existing launcher

  Note: Launcher names are automatically converted to lowercase.
  Launchers with uppercase names cannot be modified until renamed.

  Select the executable file using 'Select Executable'.
  If the file is not executable, an error will be shown.

  Enter the version number (optional).
  If omitted, the default value '1_0' will be used.

  Select an icon:
  - 'User Icons' for ~/.local/share/icons
  - 'Other Icons' to browse available icons

  The icon must be a square PNG file (e.g. 48x48 or 64x64).

  Choose whether to run the program in a terminal.

  Select a category from the dropdown list.

  Click 'Create Launcher' to generate the file preview.
  Then: 
    - 'Save' to confirm
    - 'Edit' to modify

  You can cancel at any time using 'Cancel' or by closing the window.

  The MHK button opens the My Human Kit website.
